reflectYourStyle.question: When a crisis hits the team, your first instinct is to:

options.A: Describe the big-picture goal to keep people inspired.
options.B: Sit down with individuals to see how they are coping.
options.C: Call a meeting to gather everyone's input on the solution.
options.D: Jump in and show them exactly how to fix it by doing it yourself.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you prefer to give feedback?

options.A: By reminding them how their work fits into the long-term mission.
options.B: By asking them how they think they can improve their own skills.
options.C: By discussing it openly as a group to reach a consensus.
options.D: By pointing out high-performance gaps and demanding immediate results.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your team is stuck on a complex strategic decision. You:

options.A: Provide a clear 'North Star' and let them find the path.
options.B: Use it as a 'teachable moment' to build their problem-solving skills.
options.C: Facilitate a brainstorm where every voice has equal weight.
options.D: Set a tight deadline and provide the specific data they must use.

reflectYourStyle.question: What does 'Success' look like to you at the end of a project?

options.A: We realized a bold new vision that didn't exist before.
options.B: Every team member grew personally and professionally.
options.C: The team feels a deep sense of ownership and agreement.
options.D: We hit every KPI ahead of schedule with zero errors.

reflectYourStyle.question: A high-potential employee is underperforming. Your approach is:

options.A: Re-align them with the 'Why' behind their role.
options.B: Spend 1-on-1 time helping them unlock their own roadblocks.
options.C: Ask the team how they can support this person.
options.D: Give them a 'stretch goal' and hold them strictly accountable.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a meeting, you find yourself doing mostly:

options.A: Painting a picture of the future.
options.B: Asking open-ended questions.
options.C: Listening and summarizing the group's thoughts.
options.D: Giving directions and setting the tempo.

reflectYourStyle.question: When planning for next year, you focus most on:

options.B: Succession planning and talent development.
options.C: Building a culture of collaboration and trust.
options.D: Operational excellence and efficiency gains.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your team would describe your leadership 'Presence' as:

options.B: Supportive and Empathic.
options.C: Inclusive and Fair.
options.D: Intense and Result-Oriented.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you handle 'Systems Thinking' in your organization?

options.A: I look for how the brand image impacts the market system.
options.B: I look at how the human relationships affect the workflow.
options.C: I look at how democratic participation improves the whole system.
options.D: I look for bottlenecks in the execution chain.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your ultimate goal as a leader is to:

options.B: Be a developer of people.
options.C: Be a builder of communities.
options.D: Be a driver of performance.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a team member disagrees with you, you:

options.A: Explain the long-term benefits of your vision.
options.B: Ask them to explain their perspective to help them learn.
options.C: Seek a middle ground that everyone can live with.
options.D: State that the decision is final to maintain momentum.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you define accountability?

options.A: Living up to the organization's core values.
options.B: Taking responsibility for one's own professional growth.
options.C: Being answerable to the rest of the team.
options.D: Meeting specific, pre-defined metrics and targets.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the best way to motivate a discouraged team?

options.A: Tell a compelling story about the impact of their work.
options.B: Have 1-on-1 heart-to-hearts to build confidence.
options.C: Hold a group session to let everyone vent and regroup.
options.D: Set a small, easy-to-win goal to get a 'quick victory'.

reflectYourStyle.question: During a strategic pivot, you focus on:

options.A: Finding the 'New North' for the organization.
options.B: Ensuring no one feels left behind by the change.
options.C: Getting a vote on the new direction.
options.D: The speed of the transition to avoid lag.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your approach to delegation is:

options.A: Give them the goal, not the instructions.
options.B: Give them tasks that will challenge their current skills.
options.C: Give the task to whoever the group thinks is best.
options.D: Give precise instructions and a strict deadline.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you enter a room, you want people to feel:

options.B: Heard and understood.
options.C: Like they are part of a team.
options.D: Ready to get to work immediately.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a performance review, you spend the most time on:

options.B: Asking what they want to learn next.
options.C: Discussing team dynamics and relationships.
options.D: Reviewing hard data and last quarter's results.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you handle 'office politics'?

options.A: I ignore it and focus on the mission.
options.B: I help people coach their way through the tension.
options.C: I bring everyone together to discuss it openly.
options.D: I shut it down if it interferes with output.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the most important part of a meeting?

options.B: The chance for individual growth.
options.C: The moment of consensus.
options.D: The action items and deadlines.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you need to hire someone, you look for:

options.B: Willingness to learn and be coached.
options.C: Ability to collaborate and fit the culture.
options.D: A proven track record of high-speed results.

reflectYourStyle.question: If you have to let someone go, you focus on:

options.A: Ensuring it's the right move for the brand's future.
options.B: Treating them with dignity and helping their transition.
options.C: Discussing the impact on the team's morale.
options.D: Following the legal and performance protocol perfectly.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you handle information sharing?

options.A: I share what is necessary to maintain the vision.
options.B: I share what will help people grow.
options.C: I share everything to maintain transparency.
options.D: I share what is needed to get the job done.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your view on 'Risk' is:

options.B: A great way for people to learn from mistakes.
options.C: Something to be discussed as a group.
options.D: Something to be calculated and minimized.

reflectYourStyle.question: When someone does a great job, you:

options.A: Publicly highlight how they embody the mission.
options.B: Private 1-on-1 praise focusing on their growth.
options.C: Ask the team to celebrate them together.
options.D: Give them a reward and a bigger challenge.

reflectYourStyle.question: You believe leadership is mostly about:

options.B: Development.
options.C: Consensus.
options.D: Execution.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you respond to a missed deadline?

options.A: Ask if the goal is still worth pursuing.
options.B: Ask what skills the person needs to improve.
options.C: Ask the team how they can help catch up.
options.D: Express disappointment and demand a fix.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a new technology arrives, you:

options.A: Imagine how it changes the whole industry.
options.B: Ask who wants to learn it first.
options.C: Discuss as a group if it fits the workflow.
options.D: Analyze if it increases speed and ROI.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a crisis, your communication style is:

options.B: Calm and supportive.
options.C: Open and listening.
options.D: Direct and command-based.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a team is 'drifting', you:

options.B: Coach the individuals who are lagging.
options.C: Hold a 'Reset' meeting for everyone.
options.D: Increase the frequency of reporting.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you view your role in the organization?

options.B: The Gardener.
options.C: The Facilitator.
options.D: The Engine.

reflectYourStyle.question: You think personal growth is:

options.A: Driven by a clear future goal.
options.B: The core of the professional journey.
options.C: Enhanced by team collaboration.
options.D: Measured by performance outcomes.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you have a 'gut feeling', you:

options.A: Follow it and bring the team along.
options.B: Test it by coaching others to see it.
options.C: Propose it to the team for debate.
options.D: Look for data to prove or disprove it.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the biggest waste of time?

options.B: Stunted potential.
options.C: Hidden agendas.
options.D: Inefficiency.

reflectYourStyle.question: When someone is 'stuck', you ask:

options.A: Where do you want to go?
options.B: What are you afraid of?
options.C: What does the team need?
options.D: What is the next step?

reflectYourStyle.question: Your ideal team culture is:

options.B: Safe and developmental.
options.C: Transparent and fair.
options.D: Fast and flawless.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you manage your time?

options.A: I focus on the high-impact future stuff.
options.B: I focus on people and coaching.
options.C: I focus on coordination and alignment.
options.D: I focus on the schedule and tasks.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you disagree with your boss, you:

options.B: Try to coach them to see your view.
options.C: Discuss it with peers first.
options.D: Present data to show why they are wrong.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is your best leadership habit?

options.B: Listening.
options.C: Inclusive planning.
options.D: Setting high bars.

reflectYourStyle.question: When a project ends, you first think of:

options.B: Who learned what.
options.C: How the team feels.
options.D: The final numbers.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you define 'Leadership Presence'?

options.A: Being the most inspiring person in the room.
options.B: Being the most observant person in the room.
options.C: Being the most approachable person in the room.
options.D: Being the most competent person in the room.

reflectYourStyle.question: If you could change one thing in business, it would be:

options.B: The waste of talent.
options.C: The siloed communication.
options.D: The slow pace of change.

reflectYourStyle.question: How do you start your day?

options.B: Thinking about the people I'll meet.
options.C: Checking in with the team.
options.D: Reviewing my to-do list.

reflectYourStyle.question: In a high-pressure situation, you:

options.B: Focus on keeping the team calm.
options.C: Focus on group consensus.
options.D: Focus on the execution steps.

reflectYourStyle.question: When delegating power, you:

options.B: Guide them as they handle it.
options.C: Distribute it among the group.
options.D: Grant it based on performance.

reflectYourStyle.question: You handle failure by:

options.B: Reflecting on the growth.
options.C: Discussing it with the team.
options.D: Doing a post-mortem on the data.

reflectYourStyle.question: What is the best way to earn respect?

options.B: Being a mentor.
options.C: Being a collaborator.
options.D: Being a top performer.

reflectYourStyle.question: When you're overwhelmed, you:

options.A: Step back and look at the big picture.
options.B: Ask for coaching from a peer.
options.C: Delegate and align with the team.
options.D: Work harder and faster.

reflectYourStyle.question: What motivates you most?

options.B: Impact.
options.C: Involvement.
options.D: Achievement.

reflectYourStyle.question: Your leadership 'legacy' will be:

options.B: The people I grew.
options.C: The culture I built.
options.D: The results I delivered.

reflectYourStyle.question: Finally, you view leadership as a:

options.B: Service.
options.C: Duty.
options.D: Skill.

clarityAssessment.context: Your inbox on Monday morning contains 24 items. You have one hour before your first meeting.

items.label: Message from CEO about Q3 strategy shift
Auto-reply confirming a calendar invite
Customer complaint escalated by Sales
System-generated weekly report (unread for 3 weeks)
Team member flagging a critical dependency risk
Newsletter from an industry publication
Vendor invoice approval request
Competitor just launched a new product — alert from your tracker

clarityAssessment.context: Your leadership team is in a 90-minute strategy session. The following topics are raised.

items.label: We need to redesign the office layout
Our top performer has had 3 missed deadlines this quarter
The team-building event needs a new venue
Revenue from our core product dropped 12% last month
We should update the team WhatsApp group icon
A key client is asking for capability we don't yet have
Last year's team photo needs reframing
Two business units are working on duplicate initiatives

clarityAssessment.context: You are reviewing your organisation's strategic health. These signals come in over a single week.

items.label: Employee satisfaction survey shows rising disengagement in R&D
Printer on floor 3 keeps jamming
Your CFO quietly mentions cash runway is tightening
The office coffee machine needs servicing
Three senior people had informal chats about 'looking elsewhere'
Someone forgot to stock the stationery cupboard
A major industry conference is happening next month
The meeting room booking system has a glitch

clarityAssessment.context: You are a senior leader preparing next year's operating plan. Data from across the business arrives.

items.label: The marketing team's slide deck font is inconsistent
Customer retention rate has dropped for the third consecutive quarter
A junior analyst flagged an anomaly in the unit economics model
The company intranet homepage looks outdated
A new regulation in your sector takes effect in 60 days
Someone asked for a new screensaver for the lobby screen
Your most profitable product line has stagnant NPS for 6 months
The meeting notes template needs a footer update

clarityAssessment.context: You are leading a high-stakes product launch in 6 weeks. These updates arrive from across the team.

items.label: Legal hasn't signed off on the product name yet
The launch party venue still needs a final headcount
QA found a repeatable crash in the core user flow
Press release needs a comma changed
The head of sales says the key enterprise demo account went cold
Someone wants to change the slide background colour for the launch deck
Manufacturing partner flagged a 3-week supply delay
A team member wants to adjust the font size in the product spec doc

clarityAssessment.context: You spend an afternoon reviewing your team's work and communication. The following surface.

items.label: A high-performer mentioned feeling unchallenged in a 1-on-1
Someone used the wrong bullet style in a deck
Your team's delivery velocity has slowed 30% over 2 months
The team lunch order had the wrong sandwich for one person
Cross-functional friction has resurfaced between two leads
The Zoom background used in last week's call looked unprofessional
A key project has no clear owner after last month's reorg
Someone submitted expenses 2 days late

clarityAssessment.context: You are in a board review. The following points come up during the conversation.

items.label: The colour scheme of the investor deck could be more 'modern'
A board member quietly asks if leadership succession plans are in place
The executive team's headshots on the website look dated
Revenue concentration in two clients represents 60% of total income
A board member noted that company culture wasn't mentioned in the CEO's update
The font size on the appendix slides is too small
Your top clients are starting to ask about your AI roadmap
The agenda was circulated 30 minutes later than planned

clarityAssessment.context: You are coaching a direct report who has just been promoted. These observations come across.

items.label: They are still doing their old job instead of delegating it
Their desk is not as tidy as before the promotion
Their direct reports are unclear who makes decisions on the team
Their presentation slides use two different fonts
They have avoided giving difficult feedback to a peer twice now
They didn't respond to a Slack message within an hour
Their team's morale has dipped noticeably over the last month
Their weekly report is submitted on Thursdays instead of Fridays

clarityAssessment.context: You are scanning the external environment for strategic threats and opportunities.

items.label: A direct competitor just announced a 40% price cut
Your LinkedIn company page hasn't been updated in two months
A technology shift is making your current product architecture harder to scale
The office kitchen fridge needs cleaning
Your top three talent sources are drying up
The company blog has fewer posts this quarter than last
A startup in your space just received Series B funding
The team's Slack channel has fewer GIFs lately

clarityAssessment.context: You are doing a mid-year review of your own leadership priorities.

items.label: You have said yes to every meeting request this quarter
Your calendar background colour is the default grey
Your most important strategic goal has had no progress for 8 weeks
Your out-of-office template is slightly outdated
You have not had a single deep-work block in 3 weeks
Your email signature doesn't include your new title
The most critical relationship in your stakeholder map has gone cold
You still use a 2024 goals template

presenceAssessment.short_description: A stakeholder has just held up a hand and interrupted your boardroom presentation. What is your 'Power Move'?

options.text: Hold silent eye contact and wait for them to finish before continuing.
options.label: A

options.text: Address them directly: 'I’ll cover that in two slides; let me finish this data flow.'
options.label: B

options.text: Stop speaking immediately and look down at your notes until they are done.
options.label: C

options.text: Raise your voice slightly to speak over them and finish your point quickly.
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: You are giving a virtual keynote. Your 'Audience Gallery' view is a wall of 'Cameras Off' and one person yawning. Your response?

options.text: Pause the presentation, stop sharing the screen, and ask a direct provocative question.
options.label: A

options.text: Power through the presentation as fast as possible to get it over with.
options.label: B

options.text: Call out the energy: 'It looks like we’ve hit a wall. Let's pivot to discussion.'
options.label: C

options.text: Ignore the cameras and keep speaking with high enthusiasm to an empty screen.
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: A junior gatekeeper is blocking your path to the elevator with an aggressive, 'loaded' rumor about your project. Other employees are watching. You:

options.text: Keep walking to the elevator while giving a polite, vague response.
options.label: A

options.text: Stop, turn your full body, make direct eye contact: 'Let's book 10 minutes tomorrow.'
options.label: B

options.text: Give a detailed, transparent explanation right there in the hallway.
options.label: C

options.text: Smile and pivot: 'That's a clever rumor. How is the temperature on the floor?'
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: Your team brainstorm has stalled. Everyone looks drained and defeated. The clock is late. You are the marker-holder. You:

options.text: Sit down with them, lower your voice, and share a story of a major setback you once overcame.
options.label: A

options.text: Point to the clock and demand three new ideas from the team before anyone can leave.
options.label: B

options.text: Start writing a 'North Star' vision word on the board to re-center the 'Why'.
options.label: C

options.text: Ask for a volunteer to take the marker and lead the next 10 minutes.
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: You are on stage at a large conference. A spotlight is on you. Someone in the front row has just asked a very aggressive, personal question about your integrity. You:

options.text: Take a slow, deliberate sip of water to control the tempo of the room.
options.label: A

options.text: Immediately launch into a list of your achievements and values to defend yourself.
options.label: B

options.text: Lean forward, lower your voice, and make eye contact: 'I appreciate the courage to ask that.'
options.label: C

options.text: Look at the moderator and ask them to move to the next question.
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: The CFO has abruptly closed their laptop during your budget pitch, signaling a 'NO'. The room is waiting for your reaction. You:

options.text: Nod, wait 3 seconds in silence, and say: 'I understand the initial 'No'. Let's find the middle ground.'
options.label: A

options.text: Start re-pitching the ROI data more aggressively with a faster speaking pace.
options.label: B

options.text: Close your notebook slowly: 'Fair point on the timing. Let's schedule a deep-dive next week.'
options.label: C

options.text: Immediately agree and say: 'You're right, the numbers need more work.'
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: 24 hours before a launch, your co-founder finds a critical bug that requires a delay. Anxiety is high. You:

options.text: Deep breath, calm face: 'This is why we built contingency. Let's activate the communication plan.'
options.label: A

options.text: Angrily ask: 'How did we miss this? Who checked the code?'
options.label: B

options.text: Immediately start working on the code yourself without delegation.
options.label: C

options.text: Call the main investor to ask for permission to delay.
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: You are being ambushed by journalists about an organization integrity issue. A microphone is in your face. You:

options.text: Keep walking at a steady pace, looking straight ahead with a neutral face.
options.label: A

options.text: Cover your face with your hand and run to the car.
options.label: B

options.text: Stop, look at the camera: 'We are conducting an audit and will release a statement on Friday.'
options.label: C

options.text: Shout back: 'This is fake news!'
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: During a sensitive 1-on-1 performance review, a high-potential employee starts crying. You:

options.text: Sit perfectly still, hand a tissue slowly, and wait for them to finish (Hold Space).
options.label: A

options.text: Immediately stop the feedback and start reassuring them they are doing a great job.
options.label: B

options.text: Tell them: 'It's okay, tears are natural. Let's talk about the next steps when you are ready.'
options.label: C

options.text: Look at the time and say: 'We should reschedule this for tomorrow.'
options.label: D

presenceAssessment.short_description: You have just pitched a high-risk, $10M deal to a closed group of senior stakeholders. The room is dead silent, waiting for your final posture. You:

options.text: Stand tall, maintain silent, calm eye contact with the key decision-maker.
options.label: A

options.text: Start over-explaining the 'risk' section because you are worried about the silence.
options.label: B

options.text: Lean back, put your hands behind your head to show 'Confidence'.
options.label: C

options.text: Break the silence: 'I know it’s risky, but we should probably do it.'
options.label: D

systemsAssessment.scenario: A top-performing Project Manager has missed three consecutive deadlines and team morale has dropped.

domains.name: Operations & Workflow
domains.description: Check process bottlenecks, handoff delays, and workload flow.

domains.name: HR & Individual Capability
domains.description: Assess individual skill gaps, motivation, or personal constraints.

domains.name: Strategic Alignment
domains.description: Verify whether priorities and project scope are realistic.

domains.name: Cultural Health
domains.description: Evaluate burnout, psychological safety, and team trust.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Customer churn spikes after launching a new pricing model.

domains.name: Customer Value Perception
domains.description: Assess whether pricing still reflects perceived value.

domains.name: Go-To-Market Messaging
domains.description: Review how price changes were communicated to segments.

domains.name: Sales Execution
domains.description: Check discounting behavior and negotiation quality.

domains.name: Finance Controls
domains.description: Inspect margin protection constraints and guardrails.

systemsAssessment.scenario: A critical product launch is delayed even though each team says they completed their tasks.

domains.name: Cross-Functional Dependencies
domains.description: Identify hidden inter-team sequencing issues.

domains.name: Leadership Communication
domains.description: Assess clarity of ownership and escalation channels.

domains.name: Individual Accountability
domains.description: Check missed commitments at role level.

domains.name: Planning Cadence
domains.description: Evaluate planning horizon, risk reviews, and readiness gates.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Employee engagement scores decline while business targets are still being met.

domains.name: Cultural Health
domains.description: Evaluate leadership climate and emotional load.

domains.name: Performance Systems
domains.description: Review whether KPIs are driving unhealthy behavior.

domains.name: Manager Capability
domains.description: Assess coaching quality and local leadership skill.

domains.name: Workload Design
domains.description: Check structural overcommitment and resource strain.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Revenue is flat despite increased marketing spend.

domains.name: Funnel Conversion System
domains.description: Analyze conversion drop-off points end-to-end.

domains.name: Channel Mix Strategy
domains.description: Validate channel fit with buyer behavior.

domains.name: Sales Enablement
domains.description: Check if leads are nurtured and qualified properly.

domains.name: Campaign Execution
domains.description: Inspect creative quality and tactical setup.

systemsAssessment.scenario: A high-potential leader is suddenly underperforming after a reorganization.

domains.name: Role Design & Span
domains.description: Assess whether the new role is structurally feasible.

domains.name: Strategic Clarity
domains.description: Check if priorities and decision rights are clear.

domains.name: HR & Coaching Support
domains.description: Review support for transition and capability building.

domains.name: Team Dynamics
domains.description: Evaluate trust and alignment in the new team context.

systemsAssessment.scenario: A major client escalates recurring quality issues although audits show compliance.

domains.name: Voice of Customer Loop
domains.description: Examine feedback capture and response system.

domains.name: Operational Variability
domains.description: Check process consistency across delivery points.

domains.name: Account Leadership
domains.description: Assess relationship management and expectation setting.

domains.name: Quality Governance
domains.description: Review metrics, thresholds, and corrective actions.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Innovation pipeline slows down after introducing stricter approval controls.

domains.name: Governance Design
domains.description: Assess whether controls are choking idea velocity.

domains.name: Risk Appetite Strategy
domains.description: Review leadership stance on experimentation.

domains.name: Incentive System
domains.description: Check if rewards favor execution over innovation.

domains.name: Individual Creativity
domains.description: Evaluate personal ideation capability.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Two teams repeatedly duplicate work on similar initiatives.

domains.name: Portfolio Governance
domains.description: Validate visibility and steering across initiatives.

domains.name: Information Architecture
domains.description: Check if plans and ownership are discoverable.

domains.name: Leadership Alignment
domains.description: Assess alignment across department heads.

domains.name: Individual Discipline
domains.description: Review personal planning habits.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Attrition rises in a profitable business unit while compensation remains above market.

domains.name: Managerial Climate
domains.description: Evaluate quality of day-to-day leadership.

domains.name: Career System Design
domains.description: Check growth pathways and internal mobility.

domains.name: Workload Sustainability
domains.description: Assess pressure, pace, and recovery cycles.

domains.name: Compensation Policy
domains.description: Re-check pay equity and structure.

systemsAssessment.scenario: A digital transformation program is behind schedule and over budget.

domains.name: Program Architecture
domains.description: Assess sequencing, milestones, and integration complexity.

domains.name: Change Adoption System
domains.description: Evaluate readiness and user behavior change.

domains.name: Vendor Ecosystem
domains.description: Review vendor dependencies and governance.

domains.name: Individual Skills
domains.description: Check training and capability of specific members.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Customer support tickets are increasing despite a recent process automation rollout.

domains.name: Service Design
domains.description: Assess whether automation matches real customer journeys.

domains.name: Knowledge System
domains.description: Check quality of support knowledge and routing rules.

domains.name: Product Usability
domains.description: Review whether UX issues drive ticket volume.

domains.name: Agent Capability
domains.description: Evaluate coaching and individual troubleshooting skill.

systemsAssessment.scenario: A merger integration effort creates confusion across both legacy organizations.

domains.name: Operating Model Integration
domains.description: Examine structural alignment of functions and decisions.

domains.name: Cultural Integration
domains.description: Assess trust, identity, and collaboration norms.

domains.name: Communication Strategy
domains.description: Review cadence, clarity, and transparency of updates.

domains.name: Individual Adaptability
domains.description: Check personal willingness to change.

systemsAssessment.scenario: A sales region consistently misses quota even with experienced account executives.

domains.name: Territory Design
domains.description: Validate account distribution and opportunity mix.

domains.name: Pipeline Health System
domains.description: Check stage movement and conversion governance.

domains.name: Pricing & Offer Fit
domains.description: Assess product-market fit in that segment.

domains.name: Individual Selling Skill
domains.description: Review rep-level consultative selling capability.

systemsAssessment.scenario: A key initiative stalls because stakeholders keep changing requirements.

domains.name: Decision Governance
domains.description: Assess who has authority and how scope changes are controlled.

domains.name: Strategic Prioritization
domains.description: Check if objectives are clearly ranked and protected.

domains.name: Stakeholder Alignment
domains.description: Evaluate alignment rituals and expectation management.

domains.name: Team Resilience
domains.description: Review adaptability under uncertainty.

systemsAssessment.scenario: Defect rates rise after moving to a faster release cadence.

domains.name: Engineering System Design
domains.description: Review quality gates, test automation, and release workflow.

domains.name: Product Trade-Off Strategy
domains.description: Assess speed-vs-stability decision framework.

domains.name: Operational Readiness
domains.description: Check incident response and rollout controls.

domains.name: Developer Performance
domains.description: Evaluate individual coding discipline.

systemsAssessment.scenario: The CEO says strategy is clear, but middle managers report constant confusion.

domains.name: Strategy Translation System
domains.description: Assess how strategy converts into operational priorities.

domains.name: Communication Cascade
domains.description: Review message consistency across leadership layers.

domains.name: Role Clarity
